name: Justice Democrats
subject: The media is paying attention
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Donavan McKinney is running for Congress because – unlike his opponent –
he embodies what it means to represent Michigan’s 13th congressional
district.

People from the district feel it. That’s why the Michigan Chronicle
published a powerful article making the case for Donavan:

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250509-dm?refcode=em20250509-36661&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36661_1203867_Ftcjcf&akid=36661%2E1203867%2EFtcjcf ]Headline from the Michigan Chronicle reading "Donavan McKinney Steps Up
to Fight for Real Representation in Detroit's 13th District". Underneath
is an image of Donavan outside, arms crossed and smiling. He is wearing a
navy suit with a white shirt.

Donavan is MI-13 through and through because he’s lived the struggle. He
knows what it’s like to be surrounded by polluted air, underfunded
schools, and communities left behind by our rigged economy.

And now – when the Trump-Musk administration is launching an all-out
attack on working people – Donavan is stepping up to fight back because he
knows what is at stake.

We need Donavan in Congress because he has a track record of bringing real
victories back to his community. As a state legislator, he represented the
poorest district in Michigan. Despite the challenges standing in his way,
Donavan secured millions of dollars for his community.

The Michigan Chronicle puts it best: From replacing lead pipes, to
investing in rec centers and local schools, to strengthening community
violence intervention programs, “His record is proof that leadership
grounded in community experience can move mountains.”

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250509-dm?refcode=em20250509-36661&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36661_1203867_Ftcjcf&akid=36661%2E1203867%2EFtcjcf ]If you can afford to, consider pitching in $5, 10, or more to fuel
Justice Democrats and Donavan’s campaign →

Now let’s talk about his opponent.

Rep. Shri Thanedar is an out-of-touch multimillionaire who’s tried to buy
his way into power every step of the way. He bankrolled his campaign with
his personal fortune before taking even more money from Big Pharma,
corporate defense contractors, and fossil fuel monopolies jacking up
energy prices on consumers.

Shri cannot be trusted to fight for working Michiganders.

This campaign is about making sure the needs of working families come
before the greed of the billionaire class. While Shri is an ultrarich
hobby politician, Donavan is a proven fighter for his community who we can
count on to stand up for the working class in Michigan and across the
country.

Justice Democrats is proud to endorse Donavan McKinney. But if we’re going
to take on a multimillionaire incumbent and win in next year’s primary, we
need your help.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250509-dm?refcode=em20250509-36661&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36661_1203867_Ftcjcf&akid=36661%2E1203867%2EFtcjcf ]Will you split a donation of $5 today to help build the
people-powered movement it will take to send Donavan to Congress?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250509-dm?refcode=em20250509-36661&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36661_1203867_Ftcjcf&akid=36661%2E1203867%2EFtcjcf ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

P.S. [ https://michiganchronicle.com/donavan-mckinney-steps-up-to-fight-for-real-representation-in-detroits-13th-district/ ]Click here to read the full article from the Michigan Chronicle.

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250509-36661-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36661_1203867_Ftcjcf&akid=36661%2E1203867%2EFtcjcf

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1003&akid=36661%2E1203867%2EFtcjcf

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

subject: I’m a Democrat in a Trump district -
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blue Amp Action PAC
body:
Can I count on your support?
| | | |

| | | | |

| _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T580a3b4c-0fe3-4e81-8604-be14b3e7b7e1/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

My district was just named a “frontline” race in the upcoming election! [Here’s why I need you to split a donation between me and Blue Amp today:](https://default.salsalabs.org/T06d5d114-b3c9-4ba4-bd5f-7ae30ef3b632/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
1. I’m one of only 13 Democrats representing a district that voted for Donald Trump in the last election! In fact, my district saw one of the largest swings from Biden to Trump in the entire country.

2. I have been extremely outspoken against MAGA extremism but that has made me one of their top targets. I expect Elon Musk to spend millions of dollars on attack ads against me this cycle.

3. My seat will determine whether Democrats can take back the House and put a legislative check on Trump. We need to end this chaos.

[I don’t take corporate PAC money, so I’m counting on the generosity of supporters like you to help fund our grassroots campaign. Will you consider splitting a donation between me and Blue Amp today?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T83f3628e-106d-45d6-b53a-0c4996ae3745/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)[ ](https://default.salsalabs.org/T50782a4c-df12-41ec-a93b-bc2fc6fe0a04/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T2932c784-1296-40cd-ad74-a08d7fdbff28/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

| | Peter, I was in downtown Berwick last week to celebrate a Main Street Matters grant that will help Berwick small-businesses grow and enable residents to thrive. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107401307/544571436/15868926?amount=10&source=SBD_AD_EM_FR_050925_MAINST_30DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NzIyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI2ODkwNDY1Yi0wMDJkLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KMXg3CSXh-tc2tHCADWMEsiDgak785O0J9Y5dzNDBXw=) **Main streets are the center of our communities here in Pennsylvania, and they are fundamental to our history and community-spirit.** I know that when we support our small-business owners, _everyone_ _wins._ That’s why with targeted investments like these through the Main Street Matters program, we’re seeing Pennsylvania communities flourish. **I’m proud to be on the ground getting real results for every corner of the Commonwealth.** Being able to see the difference good government can make in peoples’ lives makes all the hard work worth it. I know that together, we can do great things for our communities. It makes me excited for the days ahead. **[While I fight for these investments in our communities, I’m also running a re-election campaign. body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23050%2E4449941%2EUmmoUv) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23050%2E4449941%2EUmmoUv) [**BREAKING: Elon Musk led the deep-pocketed Wisconsin Supreme Court donors by spending $19 million on race**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61118?t=3&akid=23050%2E4449941%2EUmmoUv) [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61118?t=4&akid=23050%2E4449941%2EUmmoUv) **Elon Musk is OBSESSED...**

That's why he spent more than $19 MILLION trying to buy the Wisconsin Supreme Court for Republicans.

_He wants to make sure he and his Dark Money friends can buy any election they want!_

This is EXACTLY why Amy Klobuchar and our Democrats introduced a Constitutional Amendment to overturn the disastrous _Citizens United_ decision that FLOODED our elections with corrupt money from billionaires like Elon. If we get it passed, we can END Elon's antics for GOOD. [**_If you 're on board with the Constitutional Amendment to overturn _Citizens United_ , please join 24,999 other good Democrats in signing our petition to give them the momentum they need to get it passed!_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61118?t=5&akid=23050%2E4449941%2EUmmoUv) | [SIGN YOUR NAME NOW >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61118?t=6&akid=23050%2E4449941%2EUmmoUv)
---
Amy Klobuchar's plan is vitally important.

Here's what it would do:

1\. It would overturn _Citizens United_ - which has ERODED our democracy for fifteen years.

2\. It would prevent Super PACs and Dark Money donors from flooding undisclosed funds into our elections.

3\. It would stop Billionaires from EVER attempting to buy our elections again!!

**This would be groundbreaking.**

And it would give Democrats a HUGE opportunity to win the Midterms and take back our Majority in 2026 - fair and square!
[**_We need EVERY committed Democrat reading this to help endorse our effort to support Klobuchar 's bill to hit 25,OOO signatures before 10:00 AM tomorrow >>_**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61118?t=7&akid=23050%2E4449941%2EUmmoUv)
[SIGN YOUR NAME NOW >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/61118?t=8&akid=23050%2E4449941%2EUmmoUv)

Thank you for supporting Fight For Reform, a State Level Project of End Citizens United. The Supreme Court's 2010 _Citizens United v. FEC_ decision completely changed the landscape of American politics -- it opened the door for billionaires, corporations, and special interests to spend unlimited, untraceable money to influence our elections.
[ ](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/loc_ffr_2025_footer?refcode=LOC_EM_FR_2025.X.X_X_FOOTER_X__F1_S1_C1__FOOTER&t=13&refcode2=23050_4449941_UmmoUv&akid=23050%2E4449941%2EUmmoUv)

With your support we can harness the power of our grassroots supporters to influence decision makers and put control back in the hands of the people.

name: Heartland Patriots HQ
subject: The truth about the attacks on PBS and NPR
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
It’s a direct attack on the knowledge and power of everyday Americans.
#
How do you get your news, Peter? Do you pay to access any of it?
In 2025, almost every news service — print, online, television, and sometimes even radio — requires some type of paid subscription. And those costs can add up fast!
**All Americans deserve free access to information, especially during times of uncertainty.** That’s what PBS and NPR have worked to provide our communities for over five decades — and it’s exactly what the Trump administration is working to limit now:
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/signup/hp-email-20250509?t=1&akid=8927%2E614464%2EMT-VHS)
Here’s the truth: This executive order against PBS and NPR isn’t just a partisan attack on the press. **It’s a direct attack on the knowledge and power of everyday Americans.**
They know how many people get their news from these public services. And by limiting access, they’re hoping that they’ll be able to get away with anything they want — continuing their attacks on workers, farmers, veterans, and so many others.
**We can’t let them get away with this, Peter.** So we’re asking:
**[If you want to preserve every American’s access to free news during this critical moment in our nation’s history, will you join our call to protect PBS and NPR today?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/signup/hp-email-20250509?t=2&akid=8927%2E614464%2EMT-VHS)**
**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://act.lucaskunce.com/signup/hp-email-20250509?t=3&akid=8927%2E614464%2EMT-VHS)**
We couldn’t do this without you, Peter.
Thanks for all your help,
Heartland Patriots HQ

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/hp-email-footer?refcode=em20250509-8927-footer&t=4&refcode2=8927_614464_MT-VHS&akid=8927%2E614464%2EMT-VHS)**
---

Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce.com). Not authorized by any federal candidate or candidate committee.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.lucaskunce.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe_heartland_patriots/?t=5&akid=8927%2E614464%2EMT-VHS). Mail us: PO BOX 1240 Independence, MO 64050.

subject: Our way of saying "thank you"
body:
I’d like to send you your new 2025 Hillsdale College Benefactor Card.

|

| | Dear Friend,

I'd like to send you your new **2025 Hillsdale College Benefactor Card**.

This is our way of saying "thank you" for all you do to help us defend "We the People" against out-of-touch bureaucratic despots who reject the principles of the American Founding.
[](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/Fme-znD524VrKva1d6BbCYUKEVVPfTJia6XHDM_P4sTFnRzo4QgcG2Zv_D4vp3vaTXH_Yew01AWeQ_5R8-mTPJmymUSOdV1hB4o6uRiQKEQqC7GvMjEwdiUbPme0hrDg-vrnAtAncqvJmx_LUVsraO3wi50YTEKrBXGAik0t68LJ111PTEpMKm2hYOqV5N8LKIIP_7tt7UYLGtLJSFYfbMFhwj8wmV6Vm22JmmP1mf31xExtKJ6_7xj0bQc27edyfe_Ji8vs_O6_pcw3ErNAUKDA5TufWAodB0uoqVsNAdg8PL9XeO6G-2iEJvM3RzDjl2rKx2JabJ22TWDIulAD_iTlvMTe_FGbg3o5AzNqtTe9KfSvkxQhbrKqwa9Z9bY9geb-bPsn2u1sy9K8rAlFBFgVjNZeXjheU2qM6zAX78AhiwoNeQ_PTIcSJhka6FXkOAF6NpbRfBk/aztzub0W4TlBvl5DAUNLJrB_PftD_GCe/8)
**Your generosity helps us to counter a radical agenda in the universities** where bureaucrats are taught, the colleges where activists are trained, and the K-12 schools where children learn that boys can become girls and girls can become boys.

The far-Left wouldn't spend so much time accusing Hillsdale College of being a "danger" to education if our efforts hadn't struck a nerve.

These activists--along with their bureaucrat and legacy media allies--understand that we pose a threat to their anti-American indoctrination plan. One of the ways we are fighting back on the battlefield of education is through our **Project to Revive and Restore Liberty Through Education**.

You're an important part of our efforts, so for a gift of $25 or more, I'd like to send you your** 2025 Benefactor Card**.

**Please tell me where you 'd like your new card mailed by clicking on this secure link by May 31:**
**
[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/2025-benefactor-card](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/WY0xtw-bHrk8PPEjLhH6kgPraBW3SpPKA-4o9-VMRTY4dgA8EBVJa3d4badzT0JuieORbPV3BK4G25_dPSMils4ic_RacokT0V42kzJZ9fYxnevc41Ys3L3Usn_JJAI27ug8LnngYhkcGWl6Y_hof3TWUu1GTtVZkJGJMNT4sTxpspls96B1GNOe3wqosFHIM0O3FQ1XOyALAKJ8NeZICrbyA7Qo6CiCBSp813GDuE-VVh6meZzMr7Zrux61ZrllBZxSilWAZO8NdfPwqWAWPG4DMOcZQUSmv2h6jc2HpwmZf2pCs7RGakYwf-56JgtAUwG8vvZ3x68pFYmJbJ8H4_2aJQX-_anjr6aJ_idNlEMdgDtRBnbotJXE6wvvUWOK4xaN_Qq5VyvetpDdOCDcevuwVE38185oYoGkrG9YI3zriIoFUi20OM3hm2blwXU4vG3dtM9do0w/hKudUD6t03vQXpwLXb1TNFFD7CNRoCtv/8)**

I'll be blunt: We need your continued support because **we 're fighting against a Goliath**.

Not only are these anti-American activists doing everything they can to tear down Western Civilization...

But they are extremely well-funded. They have Hollywood, Big Tech, mega-corporations, and liberal billionaires on their side.

Hillsdale College relies on the support of citizens like you--citizens who understand the importance of education to liberty and want their children and grandchildren to live in freedom and be proud of America's great heritage of liberty.

**We could not accomplish our work without your help.**

Our constitutional system is hanging by a thread...but there are reasons for hope. One of those reasons is you and our work together is more important than ever.

We must never forget that the elite, out-of-touch bureaucrats who enforce the latest Leftist orthodoxy were indoctrinated by anti-American propaganda in our schools and universities. _And we cannot vote them out of office._

**This is why we plan to redouble our efforts in 2025 to engage the anti-American Left on the battlefield of education --to save our country from the radical ideas that threaten our liberty.**

Our educational outreach goals for 2025 include the following:
* **Reaching an additional 600,000 American households and businesses with _Imprimis_ , our monthly digest of liberty**. The most widely circulated publication of its kind, Imprimis promotes individual liberty, limited government, free market economics, personal responsibility, and a strong national defense from a constitutional perspective.
* **Teaching citizens nationwide, especially younger Americans, through our FREE online courses.** We must teach young people about our nation's history and its great heritage of liberty--otherwise, we will not preserve our country! Our free online courses (which you help make possible) have proven to be one of the most effective ways of countering false narratives about America.
* **Leading a movement to restore excellence to American K-12 education.** Far too many children in K-12 classrooms across the country are subjected to critical race theory, transgenderism, and other ideologies destructive of freedom. With your faithful support, we are returning excellence to K-12 education by launching and guiding classical charter and private schools nationwide.

I hope you'll claim your **2025 Hillsdale Benefactor Card** because you understand that we can't just talk about our country's problems--we have to take action. We have to do something while there's still time.

**Hillsdale has been devoted to the defense of liberty since the College 's founding in 1844. And we need your help as we prepare to redouble our educational efforts in 2025 to preserve freedom for future generations.**

As you may know, we accomplish all of our work without ONE PENNY of government funding--not even indirectly in the form of federal or state student grants or loans.

This independence means that our ability to reach and teach additional millions in this critical year depends entirely on the help of trusted friends like you who understand the importance of education to liberty.

**Please show your continued support today by making your best tax-deductible gift of $25, $50, or $75 --or more--by clicking the secure link below:**

[**https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/2025-benefactor-card**](http://links.redconservatives.com/s/c/37tO9YZhGcjPAmA6NsWu-uMgquPD6PNBkJJf0k0i-5QFY5R7GDC3ufL2fEmiUMJj2Exe1p_bjV3ERHwIJwIGn249cCacujVdXzsojP-DI-sEspNISzfbkF6LPf1yKZhXVJt4_-kqc8zfl760902TOGz0RGaJh-4clNH5W1bwfCHWmWJS-hbEx_Nn3YJD4KKqVutpUVMAmByf0Eix4AvKzKH2n92YlhqkeXSs-uir-IyRT7Jv8urdddcN0FMTgtXaSfl2tEpA8r39cdpPpLnc9-oAHbry087nVi_aCgEEpQgjzIT61-Vt5AWovNlPiT5sKMHNxRReIQ3EMGVDQDAW-a5iWAeU726YLWoWGNza51atXz8DAkQyI-KBT1dcmpdJzOluJu1CgeXrWH-caBd5xnONU5a8ZmvVkaKZzbsso1uqfTDR6M41lMGZlErBfUZAcC-Iv4SlIP4/fDnyuTPT4s4Yi1rmD3ub-YYeQjYKXS6g/8)

Make sure you claim your **2025 Hillsdale College Benefactor Card** _before_ May 31. Thank you again for your partnership.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844

Hello, Last month, Trump signed an executive action targeting ActBlue — the top fundraising platform for non-profits and pro-democracy campaigns like mine. The executive action he signed alleges that ActBlue has been used to _“improperly influence American elections.” _ **This couldn’t be farther from the truth. ** As billionaires like Elon Musk try (and fail) to influence and essentially buy our elections, ActBlue has been a pillar in our democratic processes — allowing everyday people like you to power movements and candidates you believe in directly. The Trump administration’s baseless attack on ActBlue shows a broader agenda to disenfranchise American voters and undermine democratic participation. It sets a dangerous precedent that must be met with even more grassroots energy and dissent. **Let’s show them it won’t work by reporting our best fundraising numbers of the quarter. ** **[Can you chip in to show this administration we’re not backing down? name: Hillsdale College via Liberty Pulse
subject: Do the courts have the final say on the U.S. Constitution?
body:
CNN claims, “There are warnings that the U.S. is dangerously close to a constitutional crisis.”

| _Please see a special message from our sponsor. There is a constitutional crisis, but it's not what CNN thinks it is.

**The far-Left wants the current administration to obey the dictates of federal district court judges rather than the will of a majority of the American people.**

But do federal judges control the executive branch? Does the judicial branch have the final say in determining what is or is not constitutional?

Because the far-Left has undermined informed patriotism by promoting a distorted view of our nation's history and Constitution--including the judicial branch--many Americans do not know the answers to these questions.

body:
but it bears repeating: Republicans in Congress are plotting to slash Medicaid and SNAP

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107401973/544573780/-1080040549?refcode=email-header&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWU4YjI5My0wMTJkLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=p33-2CXXCKtJMqiyJkTdP-T8fQYWsDwM2mbz8-v6V6c=) I probably sound like a broken record at this point, but it bears repeating: **Republicans in Congress are plotting a budget scheme to steal millions of hardworking Americans’ Medicaid and SNAP benefits to pay for another billionaire tax break.** I won’t stand for it. **And I’m taking action.** I just signed onto a motion—titled _Hands Off Medicaid and SNAP_ —to block any Republican attempts to cut these crucial benefits, **and I’m asking you to stand with me.[Please, sign onto my petition and call on Speaker Mike Johnson to keep his hands off Americans’ Medicare and SNAP benefits. →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107401974/544573781/1500447496?refcode=email_050925_handsoff&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWU4YjI5My0wMTJkLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=p33-2CXXCKtJMqiyJkTdP-T8fQYWsDwM2mbz8-v6V6c=&emci=d76b9b0e-fa2c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=d5e8b293-012d-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=5643047)** | | **[SIGN ON →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107401975/544573782/1500447496?refcode=email_050925_handsoff&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWU4YjI5My0wMTJkLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=p33-2CXXCKtJMqiyJkTdP-T8fQYWsDwM2mbz8-v6V6c=&emci=d76b9b0e-fa2c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=d5e8b293-012d-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=5643047)**

**If Republicans get their way, this budget bill will include the largest cuts to Medicaid and food assistance in the history of our nation.**
I’m pulling every lever of power I have to stop it. I’ve voted NO every step of the way. I’ve sounded the alarm on the GOP budget scheme in the media. And now, I’m joining with my colleagues in the House to force a vote to protect these crucial benefits.
**[So please, can I count on you to add your name to my Hands Off petition today and help us fight back to defend Medicaid and SNAP? →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107401976/544573783/1500447496?refcode=email_050925_handsoff&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNWU4YjI5My0wMTJkLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=p33-2CXXCKtJMqiyJkTdP-T8fQYWsDwM2mbz8-v6V6c=&emci=d76b9b0e-fa2c-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&emdi=d5e8b293-012d-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&ceid=5643047)**

name: Katie Porter
subject: See what I’m meal prepping >>>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
body:
Donate any amount to get my recipes!
# [](https://act.katieporter.com/go/21486?t=1&akid=39710%2E3422442%2EpxIztE)
Dear Derek,
As a single working mom, meal prep is not just a convenience for me; it’s a necessity.
Sometimes my travel across the state means I’m not always home in time for dinner, and my kids want their mom’s cooking, regardless of where I’m at. When I first was in Congress in 2019, and spent days at a time in Washington, DC—my kids even complained about delivery pizza!
I’ve developed and refined some easy meal prep recipes over the years to keep my family fed without spending hours and hours in the kitchen every night.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250509_1_mealprep?refcode=em20250509-39710&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39710_3422442_pxIztE&akid=39710%2E3422442%2EpxIztE)
Just like the best part of dinner is sitting with loved ones around the same table, the best part of recipes is the chance to share them.
**[If you donate any amount through this email, I’ll send you a few of my favorite recipes!](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_kp_20250509_1_mealprep?refcode=em20250509-39710&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&refcode2=39710_3422442_pxIztE&akid=39710%2E3422442%2EpxIztE)**
You won’t just be getting some new dinner inspo; you’ll also be supporting my campaign for governor and my fight to bring down costs for families.
Bon apetit,
Katie
**The recipes will be emailed to you through your Actblue receipt.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/receive-fewer-kp4g-emails?source=39710&t=3&akid=39710%2E3422442%2EpxIztE)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.katieporter.com/signup/kp4g-em-update-info-footer?source=39710)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.katieporter.com/unsubscribe/kp4g-unsubscribe?source=39710&t=4&akid=39710%2E3422442%2EpxIztE)

To give by check:
Katie Porter for Governor 2026
PO Box 5176, Irvine, CA 92616
Email us: [info@katieporter.com](mailto:info@katieporter.com)
Paid for by Katie Porter for Governor 2026
FFPC #1479597

name: Nora Ptacek
subject: Some personal news
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Minnesota Democratic–Farmer–Labor Party
body:
I have some personal news to share: After five amazing years, today marks my last day with the Minnesota DFL. | | | **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107402646/544575926/-2052546445?refcode=header&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzU5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MjViODYzOS0wMzJkLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=00kMw0uXcnm0SWccvEYoAlhTaqnN1pSX3wSs8xOOLF8=)**
---
Peter, I have some personal news to share: After five amazing years, today marks my last day with the Minnesota DFL. It’s been an honor to serve as Finance Director, and while it’s bittersweet to say goodbye, I’m excited to begin a new chapter. I won’t be going too far—I’ll be continuing the fight, working to elect Democrats in Minnesota and across the country in my new role. But before I sign off, I want to take a moment to say thank you. Together, we’ve built one of the most successful state party grassroots fundraising operations in the country—fueled by thousands of passionate, driven Minnesotans like you who’ve never stopped fighting for our shared values. We’ve elected DFLers up and down the ballot cycle after cycle, building a winning model for making progress on the national stage. Now, as I pass the torch, my hope is to leave the DFL stronger than ever—ready to keep building on the momentum we’ve created. [**So I have one final ask: if you’re able, please consider chipping in $10, $25, $50, or whatever you can to support the DFL’s work to power Democratic victories across Minnesota.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107402648/544575928/1242530276?refcode=body&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MzU5MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3MjViODYzOS0wMzJkLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=00kMw0uXcnm0SWccvEYoAlhTaqnN1pSX3wSs8xOOLF8=)

clean_body: I have some personal news to share: After five amazing years, today marks my last day with the Minnesota DFL. | | | = Peter, I have some personal news to share: After five amazing years, today marks my last day with the Minnesota DFL. It’s been an honor to serve as Finance Director, and while it’s bittersweet to say goodbye, I’m excited to begin a new chapter. I won’t be going too far—I’ll be continuing the fight, working to elect Democrats in Minnesota and across the country in my new role. But before I sign off, I want to take a moment to say thank you. Together, we’ve built one of the most successful state party grassroots fundraising operations in the country—fueled by thousands of passionate, driven Minnesotans like you who’ve never stopped fighting for our shared values. We’ve elected DFLers up and down the ballot cycle after cycle, building a winning model for making progress on the national stage. Now, as I pass the torch, my hope is to leave the DFL stronger than ever—ready to keep building on the momentum we’ve created. = | | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | = | = | | = From the bottom of my heart: thank you for your commitment to building a stronger Minnesota. With gratitude, Nora Ptacek Finance Director Minnesota DFL ** | = = = Monthly supporters are the backbone of all we do. name: New PA Project Education Fund
subject: PA Voters are Powerful
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund
body:
<a clicktracking=off href="http://example.com">
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/NPP_Ed_logo_RGB_green1-01.jpg]
Hi Friend,
Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter!
Earlier this week, the PA [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] House State Government Committee [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] [https://www.politicspa.com/could-changes-finally-be-coming-to-pa-election-laws/141393/] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house.
An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills.
HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.”
State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [https://www.politicspa.com/state-government-committee-sends-voter-id-election-omnibus-bills-to-house-floor/141399] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.”
HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.”
Among other changes, [https://penncapital-star.com/campaigns-elections/voter-id-and-vote-by-mail-reform-bills-move-to-pa-house] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers.
The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed.
The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error.
Upcoming events
As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Pittsburgh%20School%20board%20forum%208.5x11.png]
Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students.
Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader.
With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color.
When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793662/542679746/-435660973]
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/Allegheny%20judicial%20flyer.png]
Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot.
Your vote shapes our justice system!
Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager
Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms.
When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM
Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221
RSVP [https://click.everyaction.com/k/106793661/542679745/229023784]
Social Media of the Week
https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/ [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
The deadline to request your mail-in ballot for the primary election is May 13th at 5PM.
Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa]
Your county election office must receive your completed mail ballot by 8PM on May 20th.
We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location.
#GoVotePA
SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [https://www.instagram.com/p/DJbyV6DPLdJ/]
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/7237344C447445787878394747342B75584E47375A57552F745265427662334B544A4A36505A58657A5871474B7A5A6E53705571424758325657365969365747/editor_images/WCM%20logo.JPG] #CourtsMatter
This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [https://www.democracydocket.com/news-alerts/a-double-win-for-voters-in-pennsylvania] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls.
First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/445-2025-04-24-Order-denying-PA-Attorney-Generals-motion-to-intervene.pdf] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots.
The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/cases/pennsylvania-undated-and-wrongly-dated-mail-in-ballots-challenge-eakin/] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted.
After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/441-2025-04-02-Notice-Of-Appeal-.pdf] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary.
Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as Citizen_AG, filed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/1-2024-10-29-Complaint.pdf] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act.
The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls.
District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [https://www.democracydocket.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/67-2025-05-02-Memorandum-.pdf] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place.
In law, standing [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standing_(law)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case.
In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money]
The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests.
In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/citizens-united-supreme-court-ruling-campaign-finance-dark-money] .
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/EA/EA016/1/99735/images/DelCo_2.png] We are hiring!
Let's provide civic education together!
We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania.
Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote!
Positions start at $20 an hour.
Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org]
EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org]
News You Can Use
There are things every one of us can do [https://www.democracydocket.com/opinion/we-have-more-power-than-trump-wants-us-to-believe-heres-what-you-can-do/] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last.
1. Educate Yourself
Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally.
Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more.
* New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pittsburgh-mayor-election-2025-candidates-quiz]
* Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [https://www.spotlightpa.org/elections/assistant/]
* Everything you need to know about mail voting [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-mail-ballot-how-to-request-fill-out-return] , becoming a poll worker [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide]
* Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pennsylvania-primary-election-day-2025-voter-information-registration]
* Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-commonwealth-superior-court-candidates] , vetting judicial candidates [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-candidate-vetting-guide]
* Guides to State College school board [https://www.spotlightpa.org/statecollege/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-state-college-school-board-anderson-huff-barlow-black-desmarais-mcternan-sogor] , Reading City Council president [https://www.spotlightpa.org/berks/2025/04/reading-city-council-president-primary-election-2025]
* Guides to voting as a college student [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-college-student-voting-guide]
En Español
* Guía para votar por correo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pensilvania-elecciones-primarias-2025-votar-correo-como-llenar] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/reading-concejo-municipal-primarias-elecciones-2025]
* Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-primarias-elecciones-2025-votar-informacion] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/05/pensilvania-elecciones-2025-como-convertirse-trabajador-electoral]
2. Stay Engaged
To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win.
Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [https://www.inquirer.com/politics/pennsylvania/montgomery-county-commissioner-neil-makhija-emmy-20250508.html] )
3. Share What You Learn
Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting.
Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year!
4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations
Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work.
Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above!
5. Become a Trained Poll Worker
Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process.
Here are some resources that may be helpful:
Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [https://penncapital-star.com/briefs/advocacy-groups-offer-training-for-pa-primary-poll-watchers/]
Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [https://www.spotlightpa.org/news/2025/04/pennsylvania-primary-election-2025-how-to-be-poll-worker-guide/]
Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [https://fcfreepresspa.com/poll-workers-needed-for-pennsylvanias-2025-primary-election/]
6. Engage Your Elected Officials
Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise.
7. Vote in Every Election
Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility.
Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th!
Recent Events
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/61467144756E73314C4E7335774562454C6644444649732F492B6843473664593068482F7644555678714E564D50363235594F7336424671626839454D43762F/editor_images/491048556_18398781181107182_1677966645695572406_n.jpg] Solidarity for Immigrants
On Monday, May 5th, also Cinco de Mayo, over 300 people, representing 18 community organizations from across Pennsylvania, gathered at the State Capitol in Harrisburg to rally for immigrant rights.
We amplified voices that have been silenced, advocated against injustice, and showed that solidarity is not just a word—it is a force that creates change.
This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division.
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/61467144756E73314C4E7335774562454C6644444649732F492B6843473664593068482F7644555678714E564D50363235594F7336424671626839454D43762F/editor_images/signal-2025-05-05-120402.jpeg] [https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/61467144756E73314C4E7335774562454C6644444649732F492B6843473664593068482F7644555678714E564D50363235594F7336424671626839454D43762F/editor_images/signal-2025-05-05-122847.jpeg]
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/61467144756E73314C4E7335774562454C6644444649732F492B6843473664593068482F7644555678714E564D50363235594F7336424671626839454D43762F/editor_images/signal-2025-05-05-112531_002.jpeg]
NPPEF on the Ground
[https://d15k2d11r6t6rl.cloudfront.net/public/users/Integrators/04eaa6b2-88f4-43b4-b978-7bb0708f5838/356574676F576F4656484552317542704442666A614C4C4D5334613152463762514A70726B39646D4F494334727651544F3756582B436D446461684F5A536E4F/editor_images/unnamed%20%281%29.jpg] Last Monday, May 5th was the last day to register to vote for the primary election!
Our teams were out in their communities leading up to the voter registration deadline on Monday. We helped folks ensure they have the ability to exercise their right to vote in the primary on Tuesday, May 20th.
Our teams are currently getting ready for GOTV - a time of year where we are on the ground connecting with our communities and mobilizing them to vote.
It's our favorite time of year to build relationships with those we hope to uplift.
Dauphin County

clean_body: [] Hi Friend, Welcome to the latest New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) newsletter! Earlier this week, the PA [] House State Government Committee [] [] voted on two bills that have the potential to impact our voting rights: State Rep Mehaffie's Voter ID bill (HB771) and State Rep. McClinton's omnibus bill (HB1396). Both passed out of committee this week and will now be voted on by the full house. An omnibus bill is a legislative bill that combines multiple, sometimes unrelated, provisions or bills into one single bill. It's essentially a package deal of different legislative proposals, often used to facilitate the passage of legislation, particularly appropriations bills. HB 771 is sponsored by State Rep. Thomas Mehaffie (Dauphin) and “would require all voters arriving at their election precincts to show identification or attest under penalty of law that they are legally able to vote in Pennsylvania.” State Rep. Malcolm Kenyatta (Philadelphia) voiced his opposition to the bill. The bill doesn’t address [] the real issues at stake. “We need to solve problems of voter irregularities and voter fraud,” he said. “We do not have demonstrable evidence that leads us to make the point that we need to address voter ID in this way. Let’s talk about the issues that do exist. This legislation doesn’t address those.” HB 1396 has a more recognizable name behind it – Speaker of the State House Joanna McClinton (Delaware/Philadelphia). State Rep. Ben Waxman (Philadelphia) complemented the Speaker, saying that the legislation is a “comprehensive approach to election reform that tries to dramatically improve the process and makes administration of elections much smoother.” Among other changes, [] House Bill 1396 would also give election workers up to a week before Election Day to prepare to count mail-in ballots, a process that has been a bottleneck for election results in parts of the state, providing fodder for election deniers. The vote-by-mail provision has been the subject of numerous lawsuits, including one now before the U.S. Supreme Court, as candidates and parties have argued over how election officials should handle irregularities, such as errors on completed ballots, that are not explicitly addressed. The bill would make clear that county election officials are required to notify voters if their mail-in ballots have been rejected for the lack of a signature and give the voter an opportunity to “cure” the error. Upcoming events As Allegheny County voters prepare for the pivotal 2025 local, municipal and judicial elections, the New PA Project Education Fund (NPPEF) is hosting two critical public candidate forums to give community members the chance to hear directly from the candidates shaping our schools and courts. These moderated discussions will allow voters to ask questions and learn more about each candidate’s priorities and qualifications before heading to the polls. [] Meet the candidates running for the Pittsburgh School Board and hear how they plan to impact our schools and students. Moderator: Dr. Kimeka Campbell is our Senior Director of Campaigns and Engagement. She is a recognized community activist, political strategist, and civic engagement leader. With over 15 years of experience in community organizing, political consulting, and nonprofit leadership, Dr. Campbell has been instrumental in shaping voter engagement strategies and building coalitions for progressive change. She co-founded Young Professionals of Color–Greater Harrisburg (YPOC), an organization dedicated to fostering leadership, advocacy, and professional growth for communities of color. When: Friday, May 9, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 7:30 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] [] Get to know the Allegheny County Common Pleas judicial candidates before you cast your ballot. Your vote shapes our justice system! Moderator: Rachel Bracken, Alliance for Justice (AFJ), Senior Regional State Courts Manager Rachel is a Senior Regional State Courts Manager with Alliance for Justice/AFJ Action. In this position, she manages AFJ/AFJ Action’s involvement in state supreme court justice elections and selection processes. Prior to joining the Justice Team, Rachel was on AFJ’s Outreach Team where she helped create AFJ’s State Court Justice Project and launch AFJ Action’s State Courts Matter initiative. She also coordinated AFJ’s networks of law professors, attorneys, and political donors to advocate around federal judicial nominations and federal courts’ impact on our constitutional rights and freedoms. When: Monday, May 12, 2025 | 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM Where: Hosanna House, 807 Wallace Ave, Wilkinsburg, PA 15221 RSVP [] Social Media of the Week [] The deadline to request your mail-in ballot for the primary election is May 13th at 5PM. Request a mail-in ballot or find your local ballot drop-off location here [vote.pa] Your county election office must receive your completed mail ballot by 8PM on May 20th. We recommend mailing it at least 5 days prior, or delivering it by hand at an official ballot drop off location. #GoVotePA SHARE ON INSTAGRAM [] [] #CourtsMatter This past Friday, Courts delivered wins for Pennsylvania voters [] . One ruling ensured that undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots will be counted, while the other rejected a bid to purge more than 277,000 from the state’s voter rolls. First, the 3rd U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals denied [] a request by Pennsylvania Attorney General Dave Sunday (R), on behalf of the state, to halt a recent federal court ruling that Pennsylvania must count undated or wrongly dated mail-in ballots. The ruling stemmed from a lawsuit originally filed [] in 2022, challenging the state’s instructions to county boards of elections that any mail-in ballot that’s timely cast but either missing a date or containing the wrong date on the outer return envelopes should not be counted. After the federal judge’s ruling in March of this year, the plaintiffs appealed [] the decision to the 3rd Circuit. The appeal remains ongoing, but today’s order ensures that counties will count wrongly dated and undated ballots during this month’s primary. Second, a Pennsylvania district court judge dismissed a lawsuit by the 1789 Foundation to have hundreds of thousands of voters purged from Pennsylvania’s voter rolls.The 1789 Foundation, also known as CitizenAG, filed [] a lawsuit in October against Pennsylvania Secretary of State Al Schmidt (R) claiming Schmidt failed to maintain voter rolls in line with the National Voter Registration Act. The group wanted the court to force Pennsylvania to remove 277,000 registrants who did not respond to confirmation notices in the 2020 general election cycle removed from the voter rolls. District Court judge Robert Mariani dismissed [] the 1789 Foundation’s lawsuit on the grounds that the organization lacked standing to bring the case in the first place. In law, standing [)] is a condition that a party seeking a legal remedy must show they have, by demonstrating to the court, sufficient connection to and harm from the law or action challenged to support the party's participation in the case. In other news, this year marks the 15th anniversary of a landmark U.S. Supreme Court ruling that fundamentally reshaped the world of campaign finance. For good-government supporters, it’s a somber milestone. Find out why in Spotlight PA’s full report. [] The 2010 decision in Citizens United v. Federal Election Commission , they say, vastly expanded the role of big money in politics and ushered in an era of unprecedented — and in some cases, untraceable — spending on elections by ultra-wealthy special interests. In Pennsylvania, the court decision’s impact on races for state and local offices was muted — at least early on. That is because of Pennsylvania’s notoriously lax campaign finance laws. Some state lawmakers have sought to tighten rules around so-called “dark money” groups, but their legislation didn’t make it through the full legislature. Continue reading for more on Citizens United and its lasting impact [] . [] We are hiring! Let's provide civic education together! We're looking for people passionate about voting rights in Philadelphia, Allegheny, Erie, Lawrence, Bucks, Montgomery, Chester, and Delaware County to help us shape the future of Pennsylvania. Join our team and help us provide civic education in our communities and register folks to vote! Positions start at $20 an hour. Interested? Contact jobs@nppef.org [jobs@nppef.org] EMAIL US [jobs@nppef.org] News You Can Use There are things every one of us can do [] in our daily lives to help ensure free and fair elections. Some actions are small — so small that you might dismiss them as unimportant. Don’t. Every important journey begins with a single step, and the first is no less important than the last. 1. Educate Yourself Elections can be complicated. The rules vary from state to state — and sometimes even from county to county. These rules also change frequently due to new laws, policies or court rulings. My first recommendation: spend time each month learning what’s happening with voting laws in your state and nationally. Spotlight PA has an 2025 Election Center [] with all the information you need to be prepared to vote this year, including guides to the big races, key dates, and more. New: Which Pittsburgh mayoral candidate most aligns with you? Take our quiz [] Get answers to your questions with our AI-powered Election Assistant [] Everything you need to know about mail voting [] , becoming a poll worker [] Everything you need to know about how to vote and where to vote [] Guides to Commonwealth, Superior Courts [] , vetting judicial candidates [] Guides to State College school board [] , Reading City Council president [] Guides to voting as a college student [] En Español Guía para votar por correo [] , la presidencia del Concejo Municipal de Reading [] * Guía para quién está en la papeleta de las primarias [] , cómo convertirse en trabajador electoral el 20 de mayo [] 2. Stay Engaged To protect democracy, we all must stay engaged day in and day out. We can’t just show up right before an election and tune out afterward. If we remain committed, democracy will win. Check out a Montgomery County Commissioner’s effort to educate voters. It's now an emmy-nominated short film. “Running elections in Pennsylvania's third-most-populous county, Neil Makhija said he’s seen the slippery slope from election misinformation to political violence.” ( Philadelphia Inquirer [] ) 3. Share What You Learn Once you’ve figured out what’s going on, share that information with your networks and community. It might feel awkward to bring up voter suppression or changes in election laws when we’re not in the middle of a major election — but local elections happen in off years, and special elections are more common than you think. Now is the perfect time to start conversations about voting. Here, in Pennsylvania, we vote every year, twice a year! 4. Join or Support Pro-Democracy Organizations Is partisan politics not for you? There are numerous nonprofits and grassroots organizations working every day to protect voting rights and ensure free and fair elections. Supporting these groups with your time or resources helps these groups do their work. Check out the work New PA Project has been up to lately below. AND , check out our upcoming events listed above! 5. Become a Trained Poll Worker Many counties face a shortage of poll workers, especially during election season. Being a poll worker requires dedication, attention to detail and a willingness to be trained. It’s a meaningful way to serve your community and help protect the integrity of the voting process. Here are some resources that may be helpful: Pennsylvania Capital-Star - Advocacy groups offer training for Pa. primary poll watchers [] Spotlight PA - Election 2025: How to become a poll worker for the PA primary [] Franklin County Free Press - Poll Workers Needed for Pennsylvania’s 2025 Primary Election [] 6. Engage Your Elected Officials Call and write your elected officials at the federal, state and local levels. Tell them that protecting voting rights and ensuring free and fair elections are your top priorities. Even better, show up at public meetings. Ask them directly what they’re doing to combat voter suppression. Be informed about pending legislation and urge them to support pro-democracy initiatives. This applies no matter where you live or who represents you — Democrat or Republican. Constituent pressure always matters. Don’t let anyone tell you otherwise. 7. Vote in Every Election Yes — every election. That means local elections, primary elections and special elections. Become a super-voter. The more you vote, the more informed and engaged you become. You’ll also be better positioned to notice and report problems — like changes to polling locations or voting equipment — that could impact turnout or accessibility. Make a plan to vote in the primary election, Tuesday, May 20th. When are you voting this year? Who are you going with? How are you voting this year -- by mail or on Election Day? As a reminder, the last day to request a mail ballot is Tuesday, May 13th! Recent Events [] Solidarity for Immigrants On Monday, May 5th, also Cinco de Mayo, over 300 people, representing 18 community organizations from across Pennsylvania, gathered at the State Capitol in Harrisburg to rally for immigrant rights. We amplified voices that have been silenced, advocated against injustice, and showed that solidarity is not just a word—it is a force that creates change. This event was a declaration that Pennsylvania stands against injustice and division. [] [] [] NPPEF on the Ground [] Last Monday, May 5th was the last day to register to vote for the primary election! Our teams were out in their communities leading up to the voter registration deadline on Monday. We helped folks ensure they have the ability to exercise their right to vote in the primary on Tuesday, May 20th. Our teams are currently getting ready for GOTV - a time of year where we are on the ground connecting with our communities and mobilizing them to vote. It's our favorite time of year to build relationships with those we hope to uplift. Dauphin County [] [] Delaware County [] [] Luzerne County [] [] Super voters vote twice a year, every year ! Thank you for doing your part to stay informed and engaged! [] Last day to register to request a mail ballot: Tuesday, May 13th Primary Election: Tuesday, May 20th Last day to register to vote for the General Election: Monday, October 20th Last day to request mail-in ballot: Tuesday, October 28th General Election: Tuesday, November 4th Important Links: Voter Hotlines: PA Department of State Website [vote.pa.gov] Voter Protection Hotline: 1-866-OUR-VOTE Upcoming elections [] DOS PA Voter Hotline: 1-877-VOTESPA Mail-in and Absentee Ballot [] Spanish/English Hotline: 1-866-VE-Y-VOTA Fact-Checking Election Claims [] Asian Languages Hotline: 1-888-API-VOTE Arabic/English Hotline: 1-844-YALLA-US Paid for by the New PA Project Education Fund New PA Project Education Fund PO Box 453 West Chester, PA 19381 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: .

Trump's “big, beautiful bill” is nothing more than a lie. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107402929/544576925/552493051?refcode=em25_250509_medfr_fndd_gq_act___TB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250509_medfr_fndd_gq_act___TB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=300a169f-032d-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMzAwYTE2OWYtMDMyZC1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=YvfRFSt1S5juSn7sdI3zxL16tntNSAc4TdRQzPiBKv0=)

**Donald Trump can’t figure out how to lie about Medicaid cuts**

_In February, he said Medicaid “isn’t going to be touched” and then, the next day, endorsed the House bill that very much “touches” it._
Donald Trump loves to call himself an outsider.

But when it comes to gutting vital programs like Medicaid, he’s using the oldest political trick in the book.

Since his first campaign, Trump has leaned on the same tired excuse — claiming he’s just rooting out “waste, fraud, and abuse” — to distract from the real harm his budget proposals inflict on working people.

Now, he’s at it again. Trump and House Republicans are pushing a so-called “big, beautiful bill” that would extend massive tax breaks for billionaires and corporations — and to pay for it, they plan to slash at least $880 billion from Medicaid over the next decade.

**Let’s be clear: You don’t find $880 billion in “waste, fraud, and abuse.” You find it by cutting healthcare for seniors, children, working families, and people with disabilities.**

And while Trump flip-flops from saying Medicaid “won’t be touched” to endorsing the very bill that guts it, Republican leaders in Congress are scrambling to sell this betrayal with hollow promises about protecting the “most vulnerable.” If that sounds unconvincing, _it’s because it is._ Our communities deserve better than this. Healthcare is a human right — and no one should lose access to it so the ultra-wealthy can get another tax cut.

[**What Trump is doing will hurt the most vulnerable among us and goes against the promises made to the American people. More than ever before, Americans, including every St. Louisan, need leaders who will stand up, speak truth to power, and protect the people, not corporate profits. Chip in $5, or whatever you can today to help Wesley fight back against this extreme agenda and build a future where no one’s healthcare is ever up for debate. >>**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107402931/544576927/552493051?refcode=em25_250509_medfr_fndd_gq_act___TB_ettx&utm_campaign=em25_250509_medfr_fndd_gq_act___TB_ettx&utm_medium=email&refcode2=300a169f-032d-f011-8b3d-6045bded8cca&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDYzODkiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMzAwYTE2OWYtMDMyZC1mMDExLThiM2QtNjA0NWJkZWQ4Y2NhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=YvfRFSt1S5juSn7sdI3zxL16tntNSAc4TdRQzPiBKv0=)

clean_body: Trump's “big, beautiful bill” is nothing more than a lie. | = Donald Trump can’t figure out how to lie about Medicaid cuts In February, he said Medicaid “isn’t going to be touched” and then, the next day, endorsed the House bill that very much “touches” it. Donald Trump loves to call himself an outsider. But when it comes to gutting vital programs like Medicaid, he’s using the oldest political trick in the book. Since his first campaign, Trump has leaned on the same tired excuse — claiming he’s just rooting out “waste, fraud, and abuse” — to distract from the real harm his budget proposals inflict on working people. Now, he’s at it again. Trump and House Republicans are pushing a so-called “big, beautiful bill” that would extend massive tax breaks for billionaires and corporations — and to pay for it, they plan to slash at least $880 billion from Medicaid over the next decade. Let’s be clear: You don’t find $880 billion in “waste, fraud, and abuse.” You find it by cutting healthcare for seniors, children, working families, and people with disabilities. And while Trump flip-flops from saying Medicaid “won’t be touched” to endorsing the very bill that guts it, Republican leaders in Congress are scrambling to sell this betrayal with hollow promises about protecting the “most vulnerable.” If that sounds unconvincing, it’s because it is. Our communities deserve better than this. Healthcare is a human right — and no one should lose access to it so the ultra-wealthy can get another tax cut. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- = | | = | | = ---|---|---|---|--- Thank you, Team Bell = = PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI 7253 WATSON RD #1115, ST LOUIS, MO 63119

name: GIFFORDS PAC
subject: An important step forward
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee
body:
We cannot allow people with dangerous intentions to access weapons of war so easily.
#

Friends,
House and Senate Democrats just took an important step forward to prevent gun violence by reintroducing a ban on assault weapons.
Year after year, we’ve seen communities across the country devastated by mass shootings — made deadlier by assault weapons.
Newtown. Las Vegas. Uvalde. Parkland. Buffalo. The list goes on.
These weapons are designed to kill as many people as possible, as quickly as possible. We cannot allow people with dangerous intentions to access weapons of war so easily.
We won’t beat around the bush: With Republicans controlling both chambers of Congress, we know this bill faces an uncertain future.
But because you continued to demand action and support our work, Congress passed the first major piece of gun safety legislation in almost three decades. So we’re going to continue to fight to make even more progress — no matter the odds.
Adding your name to our petition is a small and simple action, but it speaks volumes about the size of the movement to get this done — especially right now. **So we’re asking:**
[**Can you please tell Congress you want them to pass an assault weapons ban to keep dangerous weapons of war off of our streets?**](https://go.giffords.org/signup/giffords_em_ban_assualt_weapons_may2025//?source=em250509-1-b&refcode2=10411.3935704.BYULtj&akid=10411%2E3935704%2EBYULtj)
**[ADD MY NAME](https://go.giffords.org/signup/giffords_em_ban_assualt_weapons_may2025//?source=em250509-1-b&refcode2=10411.3935704.BYULtj&akid=10411%2E3935704%2EBYULtj)**
When the 1994 ban was in place, the U.S. saw gun massacres drop by 37%. When the ban expired, deaths in a gun massacre spiked by 239%.
Not only is a ban on assault weapons common sense, but it’s also supported by a majority of Americans. It’s time Congress acted.
Thank you for speaking out.
— GIFFORDS

Paid For By Giffords PAC; not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. www.Giffords.org
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. We try to send only the most important information and opportunities to participate via email, but click [here](https://go.giffords.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1&akid=10411%2E3935704%2EBYULtj) if you'd like to unsubscribe. Giffords PAC was started to support elected officials who step up to fight the gun violence epidemic. Thank you for standing with Gabby and the Giffords team.

**Keeping the Virginia General Assembly blue has never been more important.**

That’s why in a moment, I’m going to ask you to [_**split a contribution between my campaign and Michael Feggans’,**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/107403470/544578581/2079212104?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_Feggans_250509_T1_1_b&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNDU4NTNkOS0wNDJkLWYwMTEtOGIzZC02MDQ1YmRlZDhjY2EiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=b2LoFhH3KGuL5EKGHelswgPnsrgVLUAZmSjI3u7lO0s=) because protecting his seat is critical to protecting Democrats’ majority.

But I will be blunt, Derek. We’re facing an uphill battle to reelect Michael.

name: Brian Bengs
subject: I’m running
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brian Bengs for Senate
body:
Our elected officials have strayed from a commitment to serving regular folks.

|
---
| | Hi Peter, this is Brian Bengs. I wanted you to hear it from me first:**I’m running for the United States Senate.** South Dakotans deserve an advocate in the Senate who speaks for them. Not above them — but really _for_ them. Right now, partisanship is ripping our state and our country apart. Friends and neighbors who used to catch up at the end of the driveway, the diner, or the county fair are increasingly disconnected with each other because of who they vote for. **We must rise above that.** **[Peter, I’m a lifelong Independent who is ready to deliver for South Dakota — and I’m hoping you’ll join me as a founding supporter. Will you sign on as a grassroots supporter of my campaign today?](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/5swtVGimTw8bKx1sZqV5AQ~~/AAUS1hA~/7vbuJLh2ywlqUhuQx4gDbVdl_xXwyrGGTyNYMA4Gm7Q0d2ktqiULXdmaAkMj_sAcZauZPwpvfC0gyeBpWBxfvOW6vziKpoVeT354uVV7c7t0JNOgM3m6EOh3QfpDigTC35-_H1Ti3v_pHDtBxu_StzxGYMcPi6s0RIFe8ApEuRRpsXW6aUh0qiV51DsAiVjjdfYvDd_TCUYJDUWYHOmZ_7tDpYhQEFpZCX7K6gFEWZWEskeCd-hBKb7584OgbcbZ31dk3XEJEGoj6_Igug43rA~~)** | | [JOIN THE TEAM](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/5swtVGimTw8bKx1sZqV5AQ~~/AAUS1hA~/7vbuJLh2ywlqUhuQx4gDbVdl_xXwyrGGTyNYMA4Gm7Q0d2ktqiULXdmaAkMj_sAcZauZPwpvfC0gyeBpWBxfvOW6vziKpoVeT354uVV7c7t0JNOgM3m6EOh3QfpDigTC35-_H1Ti3v_pHDtBxu_StzxGYMcPi6s0RIFe8ApEuRRpsXW6aUh0qiV51DsAiVjjdfYvDd_TCUYJDUWYHOmZ_7tDpYhQEFpZCX7K6gFEWZWEskeCd-hBKb7584OgbcbZ31dk3XEJEGoj6_Igug43rA~~)
---
**My career has been defined by a commitment to service to our country.** I went to college on the GI Bill, served in the Air Force, and taught at the Air Force Academy. Most recently, I served as a Park Ranger at Wind Cave National Park. That is, until Elon Musk and the DOGE team came for our parks, our public services, and our jobs.
Peter, our elected officials have strayed from a commitment to serving regular folks.
We have a president who is gutting our institutions, and Cabinet officials like Pete Hegseth making a mockery of our military institutions. **And our Senators aren’t fighting back.**
South Dakotans need an independent voice, not someone who will come into office and fall in line with Donald Trump. We need someone who will come in and stand up for what the founding fathers intended for our country.
**[It’s going to be a wild ride, but I’m ready for the fights ahead — especially with you on my team. Will you add your name and join our team as a founding supporter?](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/5swtVGimTw8bKx1sZqV5AQ~~/AAUS1hA~/7vbuJLh2ywlqUhuQx4gDbVdl_xXwyrGGTyNYMA4Gm7Q0d2ktqiULXdmaAkMj_sAcZauZPwpvfC0gyeBpWBxfvOW6vziKpoVeT354uVV7c7t0JNOgM3m6EOh3QfpDigTC35-_H1Ti3v_pHDtBxu_StzxGYMcPi6s0RIFe8ApEuRRpsXW6aUh0qiV51DsAiVjjdfYvDd_TCUYJDUWYHOmZ_7tDpYhQEFpZCX7K6gFEWZWEskeCd-hBKb7584OgbcbZ31dk3XEJEGoj6_Igug43rA~~)**
| [JOIN THE TEAM](https://go.sparkpostmail1.com/f/a/5swtVGimTw8bKx1sZqV5AQ~~/AAUS1hA~/7vbuJLh2ywlqUhuQx4gDbVdl_xXwyrGGTyNYMA4Gm7Q0d2ktqiULXdmaAkMj_sAcZauZPwpvfC0gyeBpWBxfvOW6vziKpoVeT354uVV7c7t0JNOgM3m6EOh3QfpDigTC35-_H1Ti3v_pHDtBxu_StzxGYMcPi6s0RIFe8ApEuRRpsXW6aUh0qiV51DsAiVjjdfYvDd_TCUYJDUWYHOmZ_7tDpYhQEFpZCX7K6gFEWZWEskeCd-hBKb7584OgbcbZ31dk3XEJEGoj6_Igug43rA~~)
---
Thank you, Peter. More soon on what’s to come.
— Brian
